Dental Insurance Claims

Got dental
work in
Mexico?

Your insurance
owes you money.

Most US PPO plans reimburse out-of-network dental work, even in Mexico. Credimed files the claim for you. You get the check. Standard plan: $0 upfront, 15% only after you get paid. Or go Premium: $29 upfront + 10% on success.

The Credimed Journey

From confusion to cash in hand

😤 The Problem
"What do these papers mean?
Where is my money?"
PROBLEMS DELAYS CONFUSION
✨ Our Solution
Take a photo.
We handle the rest.
Our app simplifies the chaos. Scan your EOB or Claim Form and our AI does the heavy lifting.
💰 You Get Paid
Your refund arrives.
Directly to you.
Most claims recover
40%–80% depending on your plan
You qualify if you have

3 simple requirements

A US PPO dental plan
Any major insurer qualifies
Dental work done in Mexico
Any clinic, any procedure
A receipt showing what you paid
Photo or PDF, any currency
See what you could recover
$420 – $1,800
estimated recovery range · based on your insurance
No win, no fee · Free to start
Simple & fast

How it works

```
📋
Step 1
Upload your dental bill (photo or PDF)
Take a photo or upload a PDF from your clinic. Our app reads it automatically — 30 seconds.
📸 Phone photo 📄 PDF ⏱ 30 seconds
🔍
Step 2
We calculate what your insurance owes you
We review your bill and plan to determine exactly what you're owed. We prepare the claim, documents, and letters.
Most patients don't know their insurance covers 40–80% of dental work done abroad. We do — and we make sure you get it.
📨
Step 3
We file and handle everything with your insurer
We submit the claim and manage all back-and-forth until resolved. No hold music, no confusing letters.
✓ We write and submit all paperwork
✓ We follow up if they don't respond
✓ We appeal if they deny it
💰
Step 4 · The best part
You receive your money
Your refund goes directly to you. We only collect our fee after you're paid. If we recover nothing, you owe nothing.
🤝 Our promise to you
If we don't recover money You pay $0
Standard fee (only on success) 15%
Premium fee ($29 + only on success) 10%
💡 On a $1,500 refund, our Premium fee is just $179.
You keep $1,321.
```
No credit card · No commitment · Takes 3 minutes
``` ```
⏰
Don’t wait — insurance claims have time limits
If you don’t file correctly or on time, you may permanently lose your reimbursement. Most plans require claims within 90–365 days of treatment.

Who we are

A US-based healthcare operations company built for accountability and transparency

Credimed is a US-registered company focused on healthcare claims processing and reimbursement support. It is built by professionals with hands-on experience in U.S. healthcare systems, including prior work experience in Boston’s healthcare and academic environments, including exposure to institutions such as Harvard University (Cambridge) and Boston University.

This background provides practical insight into how U.S. insurers process out-of-network claims, where reimbursements are often delayed, underpaid, or denied for procedural reasons rather than clinical necessity.

🎓 Professional experience within Harvard University (Cambridge) and Boston University ecosystems
🇺🇸 Direct support team based in the United States
⚖️ Dedicated claims escalation process for disputed cases
📋 Full audit trail of submitted documentation and insurance interactions
💬 Transparent communication on every claim status update

"We built Credimed because we saw firsthand how complex U.S. healthcare reimbursement works — and how patients consistently lose money simply because they don't know how to navigate it."

— Credimed Inc. · Boston, MA
Our story

Why Credimed exists

Dental insurance reimbursement is one of the most confusing and under-transparent systems in U.S. healthcare. Millions of patients pay out-of-pocket for dental care expecting their insurance to reimburse them — but claims are routinely denied, delayed, or underpaid due to documentation complexity and administrative friction.

Credimed was created to solve this operational gap. We act as a claims support layer between patients and insurance providers — ensuring reimbursement requests are properly prepared, submitted, and tracked through to completion.

What this means for you
✓No more fighting insurance companies alone
✓One structured system from bill to refund
✓Full visibility into your claim at every stage
Impact & results

How much could you recover?

Credimed measures performance based on recovered insurance value — not just activity. Here's what typical claims look like.

Small procedures
Cleaning, fillings, X-rays
$300–$800
Standard procedures
Crowns, root canals, implants
$800–$2,500
Complex procedures
Full mouth, multiple implants
$2,500–$6,000+
What we track on every claim
Total reimbursement value recovered Tracked
Claim approval rate after submission Tracked
Average time to resolution Tracked

⚠️ Note: Results vary depending on insurance provider, coverage type, and claim eligibility. Credimed does not guarantee specific reimbursement amounts.

Trust & compliance

Your data is as protected as your bank account.

We built Credimed on the same compliance standards used by US healthcare systems. Not because we have to — because you deserve it.

```
HIPAA Compliant Infrastructure
Your health and insurance data is handled under the same federal privacy standards required of hospitals and health insurers. We never share, sell, or use your data for anything other than processing your claim.
256-bit Encryption, End to End
Every document you upload is encrypted in transit and at rest. The same encryption standard used by major US banks. Your files are never stored in plain text — ever.
🇺🇸
US Incorporated Company
Credimed is a registered US business operating under US law. You have full legal recourse and consumer protections. We're not an offshore service — we're your American advocate.
```
🔒Your data is protected under HIPAA federal regulations
🚫We never share or sell your information — to anyone
✅Secure, encrypted, and fully compliant with US law

New Claim
3 of 4

Before you sign

Here's exactly how Credimed works · no surprises

1
No Upfront Cost
Credimed handles your entire claim for free. We charge 15% only when your insurance pays you.
2
Reimbursement Goes to You
Your insurance sends money directly to your bank. Do not change your banking info with your insurer without notifying Credimed.
3
Success Fee · Only After Deposit
15% is charged automatically only after we verify your deposit. No deposit = no charge.
15%
4
We've got your back
We contact your insurer and manage all follow-ups on your behalf. Just let us know if you receive a refund directly.


←
Service Agreement
4 of 4

Your Information

Pre-filled from your profile. Review and complete missing fields.

Bank Account for Reimbursement

Terms & Conditions v4.3

Scroll to the bottom to enable the checkboxes.

CREDIMED · Service Agreement
Simple terms, plain language

1. What we doCredimed prepares and submits your dental insurance reimbursement claim on your behalf, and follows up until it's resolved.
2. What it costsStandard plan: 15% of any refund you receive. Premium plan: $29 upfront + 10% on success. If your insurance doesn't pay, you owe us nothing.
3. When we get paidOur fee is collected only after your insurance refund is confirmed — never before. Payment is processed securely through Stripe.
4. Acting on your behalfYou authorize Credimed to contact your insurer, request claim status updates, and manage communication related to your claim. We act as your advocate throughout the process.
5. Keeping us informedIf you receive a reimbursement directly from your insurer, please let us know by messaging us on WhatsApp or email so we can close your case and calculate our fee.
6. No guaranteeWe work hard on every claim, but insurance approval depends on your insurer. We cannot guarantee a specific outcome.
7. DisputesIf you have any concerns about our service or a charge, contact us at support@credimed.us, call us at (617) 803-3831, or reach us on WhatsApp. We'll make it right.
8. Governing lawThis agreement is governed by US law and applies to patients who received dental care in Mexico and hold US insurance coverage.
